source.primary: USAspending.gov + KFF Medicaid State Indicators + PA Office of the Budget federal funds reporting
source.notes: Approximated. Federal funds to PA vary year to year (especially with one-time spikes like ARPA). FMAP rates are set by HHS and reported in CMS data. Verify exact dollars against current USAspending.gov state page and KFF state Medicaid indicators before any public launch.

majorPrograms.label: Medicaid (Medical Assistance)
majorPrograms.shortDescription: Largest single federal flow. FMAP ~52% for traditional Medicaid, 90% for ACA expansion population. Covers ~3 million Pennsylvanians.

majorPrograms.label: SNAP (Food Stamps)
majorPrograms.shortDescription: 100% federally funded benefits to PA residents; state administers eligibility

majorPrograms.label: Education
majorPrograms.shortDescription: Title I (low-income districts), IDEA (special education), Pell Grants, federal student aid

majorPrograms.label: Highway & Transportation
majorPrograms.shortDescription: Federal Highway Trust Fund — matches state PennDOT spending on roads, bridges, transit

majorPrograms.label: Other Federal Programs
majorPrograms.shortDescription: TANF, WIC, housing assistance, environmental, public safety, infrastructure, unemployment admin, ARPA tail, agricultural, etc.

riskScenarios.label: Federal Medicaid match drops 10%
riskScenarios.shortDescription: PA owes roughly $1.7B more from state funds — adds ~45% to the current $3.9B structural deficit

riskScenarios.label: Medicaid expansion population loses federal coverage
riskScenarios.shortDescription: If the 90% federal match for the ~700,000 ACA expansion enrollees ends, PA is on the hook for full coverage or has to drop the population

riskScenarios.label: SNAP cost-share shifted to states
riskScenarios.shortDescription: If federal SNAP funding moves toward a partial state match (a recurring proposal across administrations), PA's share would be ~$700M annually
